evals.prompt: I want to commit my changes that fix a login bug and also add a new user profile feature. Should I do one commit or two?
evals.expected_output: Two separate commits — one logical change per commit. First: fix(auth): resolve login timeout on session expiry. Second: feat(profile): add user avatar upload. Mega-commits mixing unrelated changes make git bisect and code review harder.

evals.prompt: My PR has 800 lines of changes. The reviewer is complaining it's too large.
evals.expected_output: PR exceeds 300-line limit for effective review. Split into smaller PRs: (1) data model changes, (2) service layer, (3) API endpoints, (4) UI components. Use git rebase -i to reorganize commits. Each PR should pass CI independently and have a clear scope.

evals.prompt: I accidentally committed an API key to the main branch. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Emergency: (1) Rotate the secret immediately — treat it as compromised. (2) Use git filter-repo to purge from history. (3) Force-push to main (requires admin override). (4) Add the file pattern to .gitignore. (5) Add pre-commit hook (husky/lefthook) to prevent future secret commits.

should_not_trigger: Design a microservices architecture for e-commerce
Optimize this SQL query for performance
Add unit tests for the payment service
